introSection: Little Rickies Little Twister was a steel kiddie roller coaster built by American firm Allan Herschell Company previously located at Riverside Park in Agawam, Massachusetts, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content: After the ride was removed from the park in 1999, the ride was sent to the former site of Old Indiana Fun-n-Water Park in 2000 with other former rides Black Widow and Rotor, where Six Flags had planned to build an amusement park. The park was never built and the Little Rickies Little Twister was eventually scrapped by 2003 after the land it sat on was sold in July 2002. Rolling Thunder would be relocated within the park to fill Little Rickies Little Twister's former spot when the park was rebranded to Six Flags New England in 2000 and was renamed Great Chase.

fullText:
Little Rickies Little Twister was a steel kiddie roller coaster built by American firm Allan Herschell Company previously located at Riverside Park in Agawam, Massachusetts, USA.

After the ride was removed from the park in 1999, the ride was sent to the former site of Old Indiana Fun-n-Water Park in 2000 with other former rides Black Widow and Rotor, where Six Flags had planned to build an amusement park. The park was never built and the Little Rickies Little Twister was eventually scrapped by 2003 after the land it sat on was sold in July 2002. Rolling Thunder would be relocated within the park to fill Little Rickies Little Twister's former spot when the park was rebranded to Six Flags New England in 2000 and was renamed Great Chase.



Light blue track and supports.
